name: Herb Crusted Cod with Corn-Ginger Broth and Baby Bok Choy
recipeIngredient: 1 knob of ginger, peeled and sliced
sea salt and freshly ground pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, blend panko, fresh cilantro, chives, Italian parsley, 2 teaspoons garlic, and 1/8 cup olive oil until well combined.
2. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
3. Coat each cod fillet with olive oil, then dredge in the herb bread crumb mixture, ensuring the fish is coated on one side only.
4. Place the coated sides of the fillets on a non-stick baking pan and bake until desired doneness.
5. In a non-reactive saucepan, cook corn, chicken stock, and sliced ginger over medium heat for 15-20 minutes. Puree the mixture in a blender and strain through a fine sieve. Return to the saucepan and keep warm.
6. Heat 1/2 tablespoon olive oil in a non-stick sauté pan. Add baby bok choy and sauté with 1 teaspoon garlic, salt, and pepper until just wilted.
7. Arrange a bed of bok choy on a heated plate or bowl and place the baked fish on top. Spoon the sauce around and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
Nutritional information: 419.5 calories and 12 grams of fat per serving
